message:
Implement daemon auto-start with comprehensive improvements (bd-124)

- Auto-starts daemon on first bd command (unless --no-daemon or BEADS_AUTO_START_DAEMON=false)
- Exponential backoff on failures: 5s, 10s, 20s, 40s, 80s, 120s (max)
- Lockfile prevents race conditions when multiple commands start daemon simultaneously
- Stdio redirected to /dev/null to prevent daemon output in foreground
- Uses os.Executable() for security (prevents PATH hijacking)
- Socket readiness verified with actual connection test
- Accepts multiple falsy values: false, 0, no, off (case-insensitive)
- Working directory set to database directory for local daemon context
- Comprehensive test coverage including backoff math and concurrent starts

Fixes:
- Closes bd-1 (won't fix - compaction keeps DBs small)
- Closes bd-124 (daemon auto-start implemented)

Documentation updated in README.md and AGENTS.md

Amp-Thread-ID: https://ampcode.com/threads/T-b10fe866-ab85-417f-9c4c-5d1f044c5796
Co-authored-by: Amp <amp@ampcode.com>

For large databases (10k+ issues), this loads everything into memory. Options: 1) Use batched processing with LIMIT/OFFSET, 2) Use SQL UPDATE with REPLACE() directly, 3) Stream results instead of loading all at once. For large databases (10k+ issues), this loads everything into memory. Options: 1) Use batched processing with LIMIT/OFFSET, 2) Use SQL UPDATE with REPLACE() directly, 3) Stream results instead of loading all at once. Located in collision.go:266","status":"closed","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2025-10-16T20:46:08.971822-07:00","updated_at":"2025-10-17T23:26:43.830137-07:00","closed_at":"2025-10-17T23:26:43.830137-07:00"}
 {"id":"bd-10","title":"Refactor parseMarkdownFile to reduce cyclomatic complexity","description":"The parseMarkdownFile function in cmd/bd/markdown.go has a cyclomatic complexity of 38, which exceeds the recommended threshold of 30. This makes the function harder to understand, test, and maintain.","design":"Split the function into smaller, focused units:

1. parseMarkdownFile(filepath) - Main entry point, handles file I/O
2. parseMarkdownContent(scanner) - Core parsing logic
3. processIssueSection(issue, section, content) - Handle section finalization (current switch statement)
4. parseLabels(content) []string - Extract labels from content
5. parseDependencies(content) []string - Extract dependencies from content
6. parsePriority(content) int - Parse and validate priority

Benefits:
- Each function has a single responsibility
- Easier to test individual components
- Lower cognitive load when reading code
- Better encapsulation of parsing logic","acceptance_criteria":"- parseMarkdownFile complexity < 15
- New helper functions each have complexity < 10
- All existing tests still pass
- No change in functionality or behavior
- Code coverage maintained or improved","status":"closed","priority":3,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2025-10-16T20:46:08.971822-07:00","updated_at":"2025-10-17T01:32:00.631612-07:00","closed_at":"2025-10-14T14:37:17.463352-07:00"}
 {"id":"bd-100","title":"Phase 3: Implement daemon command with SQLite ownership","description":"Create 'bd daemon' command that starts the RPC server and owns the SQLite database.

Implementation:
- Add cmd/bd/daemon.go with start/stop/status subcommands
- Daemon holds exclusive SQLite connection
- Integrates git sync loop (batch exports every 5 seconds)
- PID file management for daemon lifecycle
- Logging for daemon operations

Socket location: .beads/bd.sock per repository","status":"closed","priority":0,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2025-10-16T22:47:42.86546-07:00","updated_at":"2025-10-17T01:32:00.960266-07:00","closed_at":"2025-10-16T23:18:57.600602-07:00","dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-100","depends_on_id":"bd-97","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2025-10-16T22:47:42.874284-07:00","created_by":"stevey"}]}
 {"id":"bd-101","title":"Phase 4: Add atomic operations and stress testing","description":"Implement atomic multi-operation support and test under concurrent load.

Features:
- Batch/transaction API for multi-step operations
- Request timeout and cancellation support
- Connection pooling optimization
- Stress tests with 4+ concurrent agents
- Performance benchmarks vs direct mode
- Documentation updates

Validates all acceptance criteria for bd-97.","status":"closed","priority":0,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2025-10-16T22:47:49.785525-07:00","updated_at":"2025-10-17T01:32:01.027606-07:00","closed_at":"2025-10-16T23:40:29.95134-07:00","dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-101","depends_on_id":"bd-97","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2025-10-16T22:47:49.787472-07:00","created_by":"stevey"}]}

 {"id":"bd-121","title":"Add --global flag to daemon for multi-repo support","description":"Currently daemon creates socket at .beads/bd.sock in each repo. For multi-repo support, add --global flag to create socket in ~/.beads/bd.sock that can serve requests from any repository.

Implementation:
- Add --global flag to daemon command
- When --global is set, use ~/.beads/bd.sock instead of ./.beads/bd.sock  
- Don't require being in a git repo when --global is used
- Update daemon discovery logic to check ~/.beads/bd.sock as fallback
- Document that global daemon can serve multiple repos simultaneously

Benefits:
- Single daemon serves all repos on the system
- No need to start daemon per-repo
- Better resource usage
- Enables system-wide task tracking

Context: Per-request context routing (bd-115) already implemented - daemon can handle multiple repos. This issue is about making the UX better.

Related: bd-73 (parent issue for multi-repo support)","status":"closed","priority":1,"issue_type":"feature","created_at":"2025-10-17T20:43:47.080685-07:00","updated_at":"2025-10-17T22:45:42.411986-07:00","closed_at":"2025-10-17T22:45:42.411986-07:00","dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-121","depends_on_id":"bd-73","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2025-10-17T20:44:02.2335-07:00","created_by":"daemon"}]}
 {"id":"bd-122","title":"Document multi-repo workflow with daemon","description":"The daemon already supports multi-repo via per-request context routing (bd-115), but this isn't documented. Users need to know how to use beads across multiple projects.

Add documentation for:
1. How daemon serves multiple repos simultaneously
2. Starting daemon in one repo, using from others
3. MCP server multi-repo configuration
4. Example: tracking work across a dozen projects
5. Comparison to workspace/global instance approaches

Documentation locations:
- README.md (Multi-repo section)
- AGENTS.md (MCP multi-repo config)
- integrations/beads-mcp/README.md (working_dir parameter)

Include:
- Architecture diagram showing one daemon, many repos
- Example MCP config with BEADS_WORKING_DIR
- CLI workflow example
- Reference to test_multi_repo.py as proof of concept

Context: Feature already works (proven by test_multi_repo.py), just needs user-facing docs.","status":"closed","priority":1,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2025-10-17T20:43:48.91315-07:00","updated_at":"2025-10-17T22:49:32.514372-07:00","closed_at":"2025-10-17T22:49:32.514372-07:00","dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-122","depends_on_id":"bd-73","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2025-10-17T20:44:03.261924-07:00","created_by":"daemon"}]}
 {"id":"bd-123","title":"Add 'bd repos' command for multi-repo aggregation","description":"When using daemon in multi-repo mode, users need commands to view/manage work across all active repositories.

Add 'bd repos' subcommand with:

1. bd repos list
   - Show all repositories daemon has cached
   - Display: path, prefix, issue count, last activity
   - Example output:
     ~/src/project1  [p1-]   45 issues  (active)
     ~/src/project2  [p2-]   12 issues  (2m ago)

2. bd repos ready --all  
   - Aggregate ready work across all repos
   - Group by repo or show combined list
   - Support priority/assignee filters

3. bd repos stats
   - Combined statistics across all repos
   - Total issues, breakdown by status/priority
   - Per-repo breakdown

4. bd repos clear-cache
   - Close all cached storage connections
   - Useful for freeing resources

Implementation notes:
- Requires daemon to track active storage instances
- May need RPC protocol additions for multi-repo queries
- Should gracefully handle repos that no longer exist

Depends on: Global daemon flag (makes this more useful)

Context: This provides the UX layer on top of existing multi-repo support. The daemon can already serve multiple repos - this makes it easy to work with them.","status":"open","priority":2,"issue_type":"feature","created_at":"2025-10-17T20:43:49.816998-07:00","updated_at":"2025-10-17T20:43:49.816998-07:00","dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-123","depends_on_id":"bd-73","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2025-10-17T20:44:04.407138-07:00","created_by":"daemon"},{"issue_id":"bd-123","depends_on_id":"bd-121","type":"blocks","created_at":"2025-10-17T20:44:13.681626-07:00","created_by":"daemon"}]}
-{"id":"bd-124","title":"Add daemon auto-start on first use","description":"Currently users must manually start daemon with 'bd daemon'. For better UX, auto-start daemon when first bd command is run.

Implementation:

1. In PersistentPreRun, check if daemon is running
2. If not, check if auto-start is enabled (default: true)
3. Start daemon with appropriate flags (--global if configured)
4. Wait for socket to be ready (with timeout)
5. Retry connection to newly-started daemon
6. Silently fail back to direct mode if daemon won't start

Configuration:
- BEADS_AUTO_START_DAEMON env var (default: true)
- --no-auto-daemon flag to disable
- Config file option: auto_start_daemon = true

Safety considerations:
- Don't auto-start if daemon failed recently (exponential backoff)
- Log auto-start to daemon.log
- Clear error messages if auto-start fails
- Never auto-start if --no-daemon flag is set

Benefits:
- Zero-configuration experience
- Daemon benefits (speed, multi-repo) automatic
- Still supports direct mode as fallback

Depends on: Global daemon flag would make this more useful","status":"open","priority":2,"issue_type":"feature","created_at":"2025-10-17T20:43:50.961453-07:00","updated_at":"2025-10-17T20:43:50.961453-07:00","dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-124","depends_on_id":"bd-73","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2025-10-17T20:44:05.502634-07:00","created_by":"daemon"},{"issue_id":"bd-124","depends_on_id":"bd-121","type":"blocks","created_at":"2025-10-17T20:44:14.987308-07:00","created_by":"daemon"}]}
+{"id":"bd-124","title":"Add daemon auto-start on first use","description":"Currently users must manually start daemon with 'bd daemon'. For better UX, auto-start daemon when first bd command is run.

Implementation:

1. In PersistentPreRun, check if daemon is running
2. If not, check if auto-start is enabled (default: true)
3. Start daemon with appropriate flags (--global if configured)
4. Wait for socket to be ready (with timeout)
5. Retry connection to newly-started daemon
6. Silently fail back to direct mode if daemon won't start

Configuration:
- BEADS_AUTO_START_DAEMON env var (default: true)
- --no-auto-daemon flag to disable
- Config file option: auto_start_daemon = true

Safety considerations:
- Don't auto-start if daemon failed recently (exponential backoff)
- Log auto-start to daemon.log
- Clear error messages if auto-start fails
- Never auto-start if --no-daemon flag is set

Benefits:
- Zero-configuration experience
- Daemon benefits (speed, multi-repo) automatic
- Still supports direct mode as fallback

Depends on: Global daemon flag would make this more useful","status":"closed","priority":2,"issue_type":"feature","created_at":"2025-10-17T20:43:50.961453-07:00","updated_at":"2025-10-17T23:33:57.173903-07:00","closed_at":"2025-10-17T23:33:57.173903-07:00","dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-124","depends_on_id":"bd-73","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2025-10-17T20:44:05.502634-07:00","created_by":"daemon"},{"issue_id":"bd-124","depends_on_id":"bd-121","type":"blocks","created_at":"2025-10-17T20:44:14.987308-07:00","created_by":"daemon"}]}
 {"id":"bd-125","title":"Add workspace config file for multi-repo management (optional enhancement)","description":"For users who want explicit control over multi-repo setup without daemon, add optional workspace config file.

Config file: ~/.beads/workspaces.toml

Example:
[workspaces]
current = "global"

[workspace.global]
db = "~/.beads/global.db"
description = "System-wide tasks"

[workspace.project1]  
db = "~/src/project1/.beads/db.sqlite"
description = "Main product"

[workspace.project2]
db = "~/src/project2/.beads/db.sqlite"
description = "Internal tools"

Commands:
bd workspace list                    # Show all workspaces
bd workspace add NAME PATH           # Add workspace
bd workspace remove NAME             # Remove workspace  
bd workspace use NAME                # Switch active workspace
bd workspace current                 # Show current workspace
bd --workspace NAME <command>        # Override for single command

Implementation:
- Load config in PersistentPreRun
- Override dbPath based on current workspace
- Store workspace state in config file
- Support both workspace config AND auto-discovery
- Workspace config takes precedence over auto-discovery

Priority rationale:
- Priority 3 (low) because daemon approach already solves this
- Only implement if users request explicit workspace management
- Adds complexity vs daemon's automatic discovery

Alternative: Users can use BEADS_DB env var for manual workspace switching today.","status":"open","priority":3,"issue_type":"feature","created_at":"2025-10-17T20:43:52.348572-07:00","updated_at":"2025-10-17T20:43:52.348572-07:00","dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-125","depends_on_id":"bd-73","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2025-10-17T20:44:06.411344-07:00","created_by":"daemon"}]}
 {"id":"bd-126","title":"Add cross-repo issue references (future enhancement)","description":"Support referencing issues across different beads repositories. Useful for tracking dependencies between separate projects.

Proposed syntax:
- Local reference: bd-123 (current behavior)
- Cross-repo by path: ~/src/other-project#bd-456
- Cross-repo by workspace name: @project2:bd-789

Use cases:
1. Frontend project depends on backend API issue
2. Shared library changes blocking multiple projects
3. System administrator tracking work across machines
4. Monorepo with separate beads databases per component

Implementation challenges:
- Storage layer needs to query external databases
- Dependency resolution across repos
- What if external repo not available?
- How to handle in JSONL export/import?
- Security: should repos be able to read others?

Design questions to resolve first:
1. Read-only references vs full cross-repo dependencies?
2. How to handle repo renames/moves?
3. Absolute paths vs workspace names vs git remotes?
4. Should bd-73 auto-discover related repos?

Recommendation: 
- Gather user feedback first
- Start with read-only references
- Implement as plugin/extension?

Context: This is mentioned in bd-73 as approach #2. Much more complex than daemon multi-repo approach. Only implement if there's strong user demand.

Priority: Backlog (4) - wait for user feedback before designing","status":"open","priority":4,"issue_type":"feature","created_at":"2025-10-17T20:43:54.04594-07:00","updated_at":"2025-10-17T20:43:54.04594-07:00","dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-126","depends_on_id":"bd-73","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2025-10-17T20:44:07.576103-07:00","created_by":"daemon"}]}
 {"id":"bd-127","title":"Add batch deletion command for issues","description":"Support deleting multiple issues efficiently instead of one at a time.

**Use Cases:**
- Cleaning up duplicate/spam issues (e.g., bd-100 to bd-117 watchdog spam)
- Removing test-only issues after feature removal
- Bulk cleanup of obsolete/spurious bugs
- Renumbering prep: delete ranges before compaction

**Proposed Syntax Options:**

**Option 1: Multiple IDs as arguments**
```bash
bd delete vc-1 vc-2 vc-3 --force
bd delete vc-{1..20} --force  # Shell expansion
```

**Option 2: Read from file (RECOMMENDED)**
```bash
bd delete --from-file deletions.txt --force --dry-run  # Preview
bd delete --from-file deletions.txt --force            # Execute
# File format: one issue ID per line
```

**Option 3: Query-based deletion**
```bash
bd delete --where "priority=3 AND type=chore" --force
bd delete --label test-only --force
bd delete --prefix bd- --status open --force
```

**Must-Have Features:**

1. **Dry-run mode**: `--dry-run` to preview what would be deleted
   - Show issue IDs, titles, dependency counts
   - Warn about issues with dependents

2. **Dependency handling**:
   - `--cascade`: Delete dependents recursively
   - `--force`: Delete even if dependents exist (orphans them)
   - Default: Fail if any issue has dependents

3. **Summary output**:
   ```
   Deleted 162 issues
   Removed 347 dependencies
   Removed 89 labels
   Orphaned 5 issues (use --cascade to delete)
   ```

4. **Transaction safety**: All-or-nothing for file/query input
   - Either all deletions succeed or none do
   - Rollback on error

**Nice-to-Have Features:**

1. **Interactive confirmation** for large batches (>10 issues)
   ```
   About to delete 162 issues. Continue? [y/N]
   (Use --force to skip confirmation)
   ```

2. **Progress indicator** for large batches (>50 deletions)
   ```
   Deleting issues... [####------] 42/162 (26%)
   ```

3. **Undo support**:
   ```bash
   bd undelete --last-batch  # Restore from snapshots
   bd undelete bd-100        # Restore single issue
   ```

**Implementation Notes:**

- Leverage existing `DeleteIssue()` in storage layer
- Wrap in transaction for atomicity
- Consider adding `DeleteIssues(ctx, []string)` for efficiency
- May need to query dependents before deletion
- File format should support comments (#) and blank lines
- JSON output mode should list all deleted IDs

**Example Workflow:**
```bash
# Identify issues to delete
bd list --label test-only --json | jq -r '.[].id' > /tmp/delete.txt

# Preview deletion
bd delete --from-file /tmp/delete.txt --dry-run

# Execute with cascade
bd delete --from-file /tmp/delete.txt --cascade --force

# Verify
bd stats
```

**Security Considerations:**
- Require explicit `--force` flag to prevent accidents
- Warn when deleting issues with dependencies
- Log deletions to audit trail
- Consider requiring confirmation for >100 deletions even with --force

**Requested by:** Another agent during cleanup of bd-100 to bd-117 watchdog spam","notes":"Fixed critical issues found in code review:
1. Dry-run mode now properly uses dryRun parameter instead of deleting data
2. Text references are pre-collected before deletion so they update correctly
3. Added orphan deduplication to prevent duplicate IDs
4. Added rows.Err() checks in all row iteration loops
5. Updated defer to ignore rollback error per Go best practices","status":"closed","priority":2,"issue_type":"feature","created_at":"2025-10-17T20:49:30.921943-07:00","updated_at":"2025-10-17T21:11:58.670841-07:00","closed_at":"2025-10-17T21:03:29.165515-07:00"}

@@ -53,6 +53,8 @@ bd daemon --global
 # MCP server automatically discovers and uses the global daemon
 ```
 
+**Note:** As of v0.9.11, the daemon **auto-starts automatically** when you run any `bd` command. You typically don't need to manually start it. To disable auto-start, set `BEADS_AUTO_START_DAEMON=false`.
+
 Your MCP config stays simple:
 ```json
 {
@@ -67,6 +69,7 @@ The MCP server will:
 1. Check for local daemon socket (`.beads/bd.sock`)
 2. Fall back to global daemon socket (`~/.beads/bd.sock`)
 3. Automatically route requests to the correct database based on your current working directory
+4. Auto-start the daemon if it's not running (with exponential backoff on failures)
 
 **Option 2: Multiple MCP Server Instances**
 Configure separate MCP servers for each major project:

@@ -518,6 +518,13 @@ bd create "Fix navbar bug"  # Uses ~/myproject/.beads/myapp.db
 bd --db ~/otherproject/.beads/other.db list
 ```
 
+### Environment Variables
+
+- `BEADS_DB` - Override database path
+- `BEADS_AUTO_START_DAEMON` - Enable/disable automatic daemon start (default: `true`). Set to `false` or `0` to disable.
+- `BD_ACTOR` - Set actor name for change tracking (defaults to `$USER`)
+- `BD_DEBUG` - Enable debug logging for troubleshooting
+
 ## Dependency Model
 
 Beads has four types of dependencies:
@@ -899,6 +906,8 @@ bd daemon --status
 bd daemon --stop
 ```
 
+**Note:** As of v0.9.11, the daemon **automatically starts** when you run any `bd` command if it's not already running. You typically don't need to manually start it. The auto-start feature can be disabled by setting `BEADS_AUTO_START_DAEMON=false` or using the `--no-daemon` flag.
+
 The daemon will:
 - Poll for changes at configurable intervals (default: 5 minutes)
 - Export pending database changes to JSONL
